items.title: How to Save Tokens When Coding with AI Agents
items.why_it_matters: This post outlines practical strategies to stop AI agents from wasting tokens on repetitive context sweeps. Since you build with Claude Code and want to keep your API costs low while vibe coding, optimizing your token spend keeps you building longer for less money. This week, open your AI-ingest repository and update your `.gitignore` or agent configuration file to explicitly exclude build artifacts and heavy dependency folders so Claude stops reading unnecessary files.

items.title: LimitPing: Keep Claude Code and Codex Rate-Limit Windows Continuous
items.why_it_matters: This developer released a utility designed to keep your Claude Code and Codex API rate-limit windows active and predictable, preventing sudden, frustrating lockouts mid-build. It directly targets your two primary coding tools so you can maintain your momentum when you are in a flow state. This week, clone the repository and run the script in the background during your next AI-ingest coding session to see if it stabilizes your rate limits.

items.title: Put Your Claude Code Rate-Limit Burndown in Your Status Line
items.why_it_matters: A developer shared a quick terminal hack to display your remaining Claude Code rate limits directly in your terminal status line. This prevents the flow-breaking surprise of running out of tokens right when you are about to ship a feature. This week, follow the blog's short guide to add this indicator to your terminal so you can budget your prompts visually while working on your Next.js frontend.

items.title: ARouter: Drop-in OpenAI/Anthropic Proxy That Cuts Cost and Fails Over
items.why_it_matters: This is a lightweight, self-hosted proxy that automatically routes your LLM requests to cheaper fallback models or alternative providers if your primary API fails or hits a rate limit. It is highly useful for keeping your local development environment running smoothly without manual code changes when Anthropic's API gets congested. This week, run ARouter locally and point your Next.js app's API calls to it, configuring a cheaper model like Haiku as a fallback for when you run out of Sonnet credits.

items.title: Agam: Activation-Based Memory for Claude Code
items.why_it_matters: Agam introduces an alternative memory management system for Claude Code that uses activation-based relevance instead of standard vector retrieval, helping the agent remember your codebase structure more accurately. Better memory means Claude makes fewer mistakes when writing code for your Supabase backend. This week, install Agam and run it alongside Claude Code on your AI-ingest project to see if it improves the agent's ability to edit your existing Next.js pages without breaking old imports.
